raw_consumer_statement:
Below copy of the letter to Amex : American Express XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. 
XXXX XXXX, NY United States XXXX To Whom it may concern, dispute XXXX : Hello, Here is my statement regarding dispute XXXX for {$260.00} charged at XXXX Below are the supporting documents that you sent. You can see there are two traffic/parking violation tickets issued by authorized companies, such as XXXX ( page 3 ) and XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( page 6 ). These two tickets and charges are valid, I am not disputing it. These tickets have unique Ref. number, date, and time. Also, it has a photo of the violation. Most importantly, its issued by an authorized company ( not the rental company or debt collection agency as a third charge ). 
Please see page 9 as a supporting document for the third charge. This is not a violation ticket, its a collection letter from XXXX ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ). This company is not authorized to issue parking violation tickets ; its just a collection agency. Until now XXXX does not provide an actual parking violation ticket for this charge as on pages 3 and 6. Their invoice has the same REF number XXXX, same date XX/XX/23 and same address XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX as ticket number one on page 3. 
To find all this easier, I marked below by yellow color. 
When XXXX received the first ticket ( page 3 ), they charged me XXXX pounds, but they did not send the payment to XXXX, who issued this ticket. After a few months of not receiving payment, XXXX sold this case to XXXX ( page 9 ). XXXX sent an invoice for the same ticket to XXXX, but a bigger amount ( because of payment delay ), and then XXXX charged me XXXX pounds for the same ticket with REF XXXX. 
All these documents are so obvious ; I dont know why Amex makes decisions without having ACTUAL parking violation tickets, but based on some collection letter. Please ask XXXX to provide an actual parking ticket for a XXXX pounds charge before you close the dispute. 


Best regards, XXXX
